author: Matt | IntergalacticHerald
content: My faith is being tested y'all. If you know me you know I've been a supporter since almost the beginning of Star Atlas. Held on through the bear market and FTX debacle without complaint. Lately I just can't stop questioning whether or not SA will be around in 2-3 years. We can't count on another bull market to save us. As an outside investor, I would be extremely hesitant to partner with ATMTA due to the lack of regulatory clarity around NFTs, and the associated risks. Very large sums and long timescales will be needed to create the full UE5 MMO experience. I'm excited for SAGE but also aware that it will be in development without full features for years. I worry we'll run out of runway before the product is far enough along to attract a significant playerbase.   Can someone more bullish give me their take? I don't want to give up but I need more reasons to justify holding on. Didn't post in genchat because I sincerely don't want to FUD.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: Fair points but remember most mobile games are freenium.  People purchase gems, etc. all the time but the initial free install does get them hooked.   But, to play SAGE, I assume you are going to need to purchase a ship.  True, it is a one-time fee but then you need to buy resources.  So, you are buying something regardless.  A subscription model just spreads out those costs but gives the business a predictable revenue stream.   In the end, there are so many revenue models.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: I hear yah. If the facts of how the market works show that a small monthly fee on these types of games doesn't work (although I have to wonder what Eve and Stellaris are therefore thinking) , then sure don't charge for SAGE. But the point is, if you have a viable consumer product in SAGE, and you can't count on new ship sales to drive revenue (which they aren't and why more ships haven't been released it seems because we aren't seeing an influx of buyers; the low hanging fruit, us, has already been captured), then find ways to utilize a marketable product in SAGE to generate some revenue and increase awareness. If that is through some kind of ship sale in the XXS/XS category for $ sensitive players to be able to play SAGE, then great. But the ecosystem needs a product that can drive revenue outside of just large dollar ship sales and hopefully SAGE can provide that playable interface in the interim between now and UE5 release, and it attracts a large volume of new players at the low dollar spend level per player. Anyway, I'm still super hyped about this project and will continue to put more money in

author: Odicerous
content: My opinion differs with many on this board (and Signal touched on this), when they keep asking “IF” SA can ink an investment deal?  I’m sure they can.  The question isn’t IF.  The question is COST.  The question is always cost.  There is a ton of investment capital in this space.  SA could probably ink a deal tomorrow, but at what price?  How much control would they give up?  How much would they need to change the game?  A cash infusion buys them runway, yes, but it also creates new payment and milestone obligations.  Many investors will value near term returns over long term success.  Will they push adoption of a lesser product?  Will they require collateral such as POLIS, or in game assets, diluting what we have?  Will they levy additional tx fees to cover the juice?  How will they deal with the DAO?

author: Odicerous
content: My guess is that adequate funding opportunities exist, but they aren’t worth the cost at this point.  With each new milestone – ship sales,  land sales, movement testing, showroom releases, SAGE release – ATMTA improves its bargaining position.  I believe they are negotiating terms with multiple groups. But as long as they are making progress along their timeline (not our timeline), I think they hold out for the best terms they can get.  For that reason, I don’t see any deal being announced soon.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: Traditionally, to play a game, you had to purchase it on a cartridge, DVD or recently digital download.  You played the game and enjoyed the experience.  Then, the freemium model took over.  You started playing the game for free and then purchase things to enhance your experience.  The value went one-way only.  But, with web3 and blockchain, the inflection point, to me, is that all those purchases now have value outside of the game developer's ecosystem.  I see the resistance of the traditional game studios to embrace web3 and blockchain and that gives Star Atlas a chance to be a disruptor.  If successful, the inflection point to me is why wouldn't gamer expect that going forward.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: I think one other factor is we are all talking like our first interaction with SAGE will be a completed project i.e. a finished game.  It will be pre-alpha.  I think it will be really hard to convince many new people who haven't been following Star Atlas for a while to see the reason to play a "game" that isn't a game.  Most people expect a game to be a finished product.  Thus, you can't expect a new person to buy a ship to just move around a 2D game board.   This is my same issue with the Showroom.  There is no reason to log in daily.  I know we are getting pre-access in both UE5 and SAGE.  I am fine with that but I understand the roadmap.  But, I totally agree with the need for a marketable product also called a "minimum viable product".  I know it is coming according to the roadmap but not anytime soon.

author: Matt | IntergalacticHerald
content: Your concerns are absolutely valid, and not at all illogical.  It’s considered heresy at times to say it, but of course there’s absolutely a very real scenario where this doesn’t work out.  It was always there, and always probably a higher possibility than you realized, and now that you can actually see where and how it could potentially end it’s absolutely reasonable to feel the way that you do.  Let’s return to basics.  How exposed are you, and could you chose to be less exposed so that you can sleep at night and protect your mental health and financial well-being for the risks you are taking?  If you are over extended and losing sleep, fix that first.  If you had 50% less Star Atlas stuff but could relax more about your risks here, that’s better for both you and the community at large (a bunch of stressed out people up to their eyeballs in SA isn’t healthy or good for anyone including SA).  Take care of yourself, be kind to yourself, and realize that sometimes the greatest super power you can have as an investor is the flexibility to change how you think about something when the facts change, and to act accordingly.  As far as bullish things to make you feel better?  Keep in mind that SA could likely very easily raise cash from VC’s.  I’d imagine they don’t want to take a down round of fund raising and are waiting for more content to be out to improve their negotiating position, but I have no doubt that if push came to shove and they needed money and accepted a lower valuation than they feel they are worth they could do a 10-20m dollar equity raise very easily.  That could buy you another 6-12 months on top of what’s already there in terms of runway without much bloodshed in terms of equity loss and now you are into mid to late 2024.  If this project burns out, it won’t do so quietly they’ll pull every lever imaginable before they get there.  Meanwhile at any given point if you get enough features and content out this thing could start funding itself.

author: [AEP] Boen
content: I think one other factor is we are all talking like our first interaction with SAGE will be a completed project i.e. a finished game.  It will be pre-alpha.  I think it will be really hard to convince many new people who haven't been following Star Atlas for a while to see the reason to play a "game" that isn't a game.  Most people expect a game to be a finished product.  Thus, you can't expect a new person to buy a ship to just move around a 2D game board.   This is my same issue with the Showroom.  There is no reason to log in daily.  I know we are getting pre-access in both UE5 and SAGE.  I am fine with that but I understand the roadmap.  But, I totally agree with the need for a marketable product also called a "minimum viable product".  I know it is coming according to the roadmap but not anytime soon.

author: CryptoGamer [EVM]
content: Yeah, there really is no answer that anyone can give you to make you feel better. Its a high risk, high reward situation. I think if the team got their back against the wall, they would just need to lower their current evaluation of Atmta to raise capital to keep this thing going...but otherwise no one can give you an answer on the regulatory front. At the end of the day, only risk what youre willing to accept as a total loss and anything better than that would be a surprise benefit. I poured millions into this cause its a great concept and someone (if its not Star Atlas) will develop a Sustainable Metaverse Economy for the masses..as of right now, Star Altas is one of the early leaders in that space...and the winners will reap huge gains for their supporters...

author: [GIA]Strictz9
content: Hey Revolver! Imo it means more people will make web3 games and learn the value of tokenized gaming experiences   People can start making games on Unity with native web3 functionality, its a big boost to web3 just like Star Atlas' SDK with UE5 is.   UE5 is a whole generation of tech ahead of Unity as I understand it. Unity has a lot of devs though. I see it as another onramp to web3, and Star Atlas + UE5 + Solana is the highest quality product out there. So, this can be another way people can find Star Atlas indirectly.

author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content: I think if it walks like a duck, qwacks like a duck .... it is a duck.  If someone starts tracking someone down, to find their identity in the real world, after making threats, then that demonstrates intent enough for me to say, yes, get the authorities involved.  I always thought "shit-talking" , meaning threatening to assault someone, was low-class, and it never attracted me.  I think it crosses a line.  At the same time, does one comment, that is not thought out, and likely not meant, justify some investigation..... almost certainly not.

author: Odicerous
content: the officer's badge was the exclusive rebirth badge. the vip permit was a free in game item drop. i mentioned we had regrets for providing in game assets early because bothe the faction passport and the pilot license of the previous 2 tiers had to be retconned into other items due to the confusion here. do we have to do that again with the VIP Permit or are people cool with just having an early access item that will increase in supply? to me it's like pre-ordering a tesla or something and if you're one of the first down the line it has more value and you get to use it before everyone else when production does kick in, while everyone else has to wait for theirs to roll out or purchase at higher rates from the owners who already have it. check out the hummer EV pickup craziness. pre-ordered at i think around 80k or something but the first ones off the line were selling second market for 250k.

author: Matt | IntergalacticHerald
content: Yes, when people deposit an amount in a bank, and get back less than $1.00  from the bank, for each $1.00 that they put in, they do not get all of their own money, back: they get some amount of cents back, instead, i.e. " cents on the dollar".  The amount ( less ) that they do get back, above the FDIC guaranteed amount , may very well be affected by a lot of things, including, precisely how short the bank is on assets, as compared to its liabilities, at the moment that it is seized and taken over by the FDIC.  I am not a financial pro of any sort.

reply.preview: Yes, when people deposit an amount in a bank, and get back less than $1.00 from the bank, for each $1.00 that they put in, they do not get all of their own money, back: they get some amount of cents back, instead, i.e. " cents on the dollar". The amount ( less ) that they do get back, above the FDIC guaranteed amount , may very well be affected by a lot of things, including, precisely how short the bank is on assets, as compared to its liabilities, at the moment that it is seized and taken over by the FDIC. I am not a financial pro of any sort.

author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content: My understanding of FDIC insured deposits, is if someone has less than $250,000, at person will get back all their money without any deductions.  The issue is for money above that amount.  When a bank is taken over by the FDIC, the FDIC then determines what happens next.  But, an insolvent bank doesn't have no assets left but not 100% to cover all depositors.   But, I too am not a financial pro.

reply.preview: My understanding of FDIC insured deposits, is if someone has less than $250,000, at person will get back all their money without any deductions. author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content: SVB ( the second-largest bank failure in US history as it has been reported ), did not fail in any case, because of their office hours.  They failed as I understand it, because their assets were less than their liabilities and this was exposed by their customers demanding the return of their deposits that they had made at this bank, to an extent that it brought the bank to a culmination point, of financial failure.

author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content: A lot of this,  generally in banking at present, is related to the value of U.S. Treasuries, purchased at a low rate of return and long duration, versus the value of more more recent U.S. Treasuries purchasable in the general market with a much higher rate of return at various durations.

author: Signal
content: My guess?  Is they’ve either already taken a down round or know for a fact that one is fully available so they are under less pressure to pull a unicorn out of their hat with SAGE short term.  Even if you went out with a 50m valuation (insanely low I’m sure most would agree), you could sell off 49% equity for 24.5m and you now have until late next year.  The runway isn’t actually a cliff.  They have tons of options and are likely threading the needle for the best possible deal.

author: Signal
content: SA ACTUALLY burning out would likely take at least 18-24 months from now if they fired off all the emergency flares and exercised all their options available to them (even under tough circumstances for raising capital).

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: Interesting to see the CCP news. I played a lot of EVE Online back in the day, then played EVE Echoes heavily for a few months (multi-tasked in many a work meeting when we had to scramble for home defense...), but frankly, I'm no longer in the market for an all-in, every minute of every hour type of gameplay. SA still has big appeal to me given the duality that SAGE brings in terms of a browser-based game (even if it requires attention), plus the instant transferability and convertibility of assets into cash. At the end of the day, it's horses for courses... each of these entrants should bring a complementary aspect to give more choice in the market.

author: [AEP] MetaVerse Explorer
content:
hey guys, I just wanted to share something that might actually be useful to the team ( if you weren't aware already) tagging @Santi & @DominicVain only, as not to bother the rest of the team. 

Watching UE 5.2 at GDC and one item stood out. 
at 2:05, they describe the 'opal' project. 
Using this item I believe the team can use this process exclusively for the rainbow line. 
The arc, chi and om fit this aesthetic of the 'photali' and would probably cut down on other dev time.

Please watch the vid in its entirely for more context.

author: CryptoGamer [EVM]
content: Playing devil's advocate for a minute here.  Agreed, the community wants SAGE and that's what we've been waiting for, but from the company's perspective, whats going to generate more revenue/VC investment; SAGE or UE5? From my own humble perspective, it would appear that the team has written of SAGE as an internal revenue generation model for this year, so they possibly know or view the fact that UE5 development must continue in order to secure external funding.

author: 🅰🅲🅴🅺🅸🅽🅶MUD
content: building on your argument, I am not sure how either SAGE or UE5 generates revenue in the short-term.  Most of the community probably already owns at least one ship and probably many more.  So, new ship sales aren't going to bring fresh USDC into the company's bank account.  Marketplace fees are something though.  But what is going to bring someone new into the community to purchase a ship to play a very simplistic game.  There are tons of interesting fully developed games available to purchase on PC, consoles and mobile and can be played immediately for tons of enjoyment.  But on the other hand, those of us in the community for while now have been waiting for something to actually "play" and hopefully with gameplay that makes us want to play on a daily basis.  SAGE definitely seems like that and even the "mini-games" in the upcoming releases of the Showroom have some replay options.

author: Oscar GF | Starke FinanceUstur
content: Agree. Echoing Michaels comments, the way SAGE or UE5 generate revenue is by demonstrating quality of product and potentially attracting outside investment. On your second point, ship sales are like 100K per week, assuming a ~4% fee, which is even further split by DAO share, thats maybe what, 2.5K a week in marketplace transactions fees?   By rough math (I'm not a mathematician or econ guru) the Star Atlas economy would need to generate roughly 100million in marketplace transactions a month to sustain development without any outside dollars.  Now thats probably an overestimate, but that's a rough 1.2 Billion GDP. Doable at full SAGE and UE5, I think so, but doable in 2023 may be another question.

content: Nice excel, now let's think a bit out of the excel, because excel don´t predict human actions....  Let´s imagine that Sage is really fun to play and people are really taking rewards for playing the game, what happens if the people that are selling start playing the game? I´m going to give another example, Vzuz Opod, total supply: 1900, imagine that this ship help players find the rares material...1900 ships only for 30k Players register???? what is going to happen....they will disappear from the Market place in 3 days!!! and the price will be 3x in 1 week. author: Signal
content: After everything that’s happened up to this point (delays, lost funds, runway dangers), think of what would have to be going on here for that to seem like a good or reasonable idea to a new person, maybe in the middle of a recession.  Sinking $8,000+ into a virtual NFT spaceship for a project with an unknown future.  And you’d need A LOT of people to think that’s a great idea.

author: Signal
content: Star Atlas ships are at the intersection of luxury spending and ultra high risk.

author: Signal

author: Signal

author: Signal
content: You think it’s hard for us with 6 months hanging over our heads?  That’s the situation of every employee on this project.

author: Signal
content: Do it for THEM.  If you get to 60-90 days out from running out of money I guarantee you the bulk of your people have already updated their resumes and started talking.  That’s if they haven’t started already.  That’s absolutely not a war you want to have to fight in this environment and at this point in your build.  If you aren’t a founder or high level executive, your mortgage and family and well being is likely on the line right now.  Screw the investor base, do it for your people.  Get the damn grim reaper out of your house.  You need to pull whatever trigger you need to pull to kick this thing out 12-18 months at least.  It’s rare that one thing solves everything, but in this case it does.  A big pile of money in the bank solves everything for SA.  Assets and tokens would rise in value, barriers to entry would be reduced for new people, good will shoots through the roof, bull cases become easier to write and defend, the team isn’t constantly peppered with as many cash and time questions because now you have both cash AND time.  It’s the cure all for every problem right now.  Don’t be greedy, shake some hands.  Owning 60% equity in ATMTA at 2x the current situation is a win.  You need to see that.  Build, do well, buy it back later.

author: PG I BULK
content: Sure, we don't need actual sales for the price to go up, we just need the psychology to change and before you know it the bid / ask spread on an X4 goes from $$4.30 centering to centering around $19.  My point is I hope the team is not basing their cash funding expectations on ship sales coming through to save the day because very likely the dev team ship sales in 2023 will not be the panacea we need. (and I don't think the team is thinking that) One has to be more cautious in their predictions esp when the cash runway is so tight, because the closer we get to the drop dead date the fewer opportunities we have to turning the ship around in time.  It is a HIGHLY likely scenario that it will take at least 100K new players, each buying an average of 2-4 XXS to Small sized ships (remember, these are not crypto nft fanatic type people like us) to even absorb the potential supply of ships back to VWAP before the team even sees a dollar of ship sales revenue. And we need to respect those potential odds and their resulting impact on cash. I'm confident the team understands this and realizes that outside of developing a new revenue product or putting a new ship on the market at full VWAP that maybe has some kind of partnership agreement with Playboy/Playgirl (I jest), capital funding is the only feasible route no matter the dilution. I don't want us to wait 6 months and think 'well damn, the psychology of our current user base wasn't what we expected. Shows over.'

author: Signal
content: I think you make a good point.  $8000 can be used to do so more than purchase a virtual NFT spaceship.  This how my wife thinks of my ships.  Anything is a better use of the money to her.  A $100 ship is another thing.  But, I think we are missing a point.  What does that ship give me?  Like traditionally gaming, I might be an experience like going to the movies.  Then, I am fine spending the money.    But, the possibility of Star Atlas is a return on that original purchase in the form of rewards in ATLAS.  Isn't that really the difference?

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: Oh we had conversations before I got into Star Atlas about cryptocurrency.  The wife really didn't go for that either.  I tried to explain the technically benefits of blockchain but didn't convince her.  Nevertheless, I find a lot of value in asking her opinion because a lot of the resistance to adoption of new technology is because people don't see the immediate benefit or use case.  I had a similar conversation with my teenager son about Star Atlas.  If there was as playable game, he would have been interested.  The only way I convinced him to buy a ship back in December 2021 was the play-to-earn.  But SCORE didn't make any sense to him and he went back to playing Destiny, League of Legends, etc.  I hope to get him back in when SAGE is playable.

author: Odicerous
content:
Yeah, to me, there is definitely a scaling issue.  5 Capital ships in a C11 just doesn’t seem right.
But if the rest of the hangared fleet is sized down within the same storage capacity, it makes a lot more sense.
Maybe, just one of the five Capital capacity hangars actually holds a Capital ship, then fill the others with smaller ships based on the existing formula.If my math is right, a C11 could be outfitted like:
-1x Capital - D9 for salvage and repair after major battles 
-4x Large – 2 Bitboat & 2 Arc for transporting people and goods to and from the behemoth
-10x Medium – 8 F4 for long range "peacekeeping" & 2 Evacs for rescue and recovery
-14x Small – 10 X6 for short range patrol and defense, 2 Medtech, & 2 R6 for maintenance support
-44x XSmall – 44 X5s for swarming invaders and creating a chaotic cloud of angry laser bats

author: Odicerous
content:
Just an example, but this seems appropriate for a C11’s day to day operations.
For purely combative missions, I would imagine a couple of C9s flanking the C11 in formation, not inside its hangars.

author: Odicerous
content: If I understand correctly, the 5 Commander hangars in the C11 conceivably can each hold a Capital ship.  Which doesn't make for good math from a realism perspective.

author: Odicerous
content: I could be wrong.  As I understand it, a Commander hangar holds 1 Capital ship, or 2 Larges, or 4 Mediums, or 8 Smalls, etc.

author: Odicerous
content: A Large hangar holds 1 Medium, or 2 Smnalls, or 4 XSmalls.

author: Odicerous
content: Or combos.  A Capital hangar can hold 1 Large, or 2 mediums, or 1 Medium and 2 Smalls, etc.

author: Odicerous
content: That makes good sense to me, except, as people pointed out, when you try to fit 5 Capitals into a C11, it doesn't seem right.

author: Odicerous
content: I think the team has been struggling with this.   They have been pretty slow in releasing hangar info, prolly because it is a delicate decision.     Smaller hangars have less impact on Sage onboarding.  Smaller ships are a lot faster to build, flesh out, and get into the game. But if hangars are too small, the long terms stabilizing effect of a C11 is compromised, and the ship is undervalued.  Tough call.  I have been anxious and critical about them not releasing the hangar info.  But i also get it is a tough decision and can't be made lightly.   It will have huge impact years from now as the game combat really develops in complexity.

author: Odicerous
content:
I’m less concerned with the early versions of Sage, and more the long term legacy of these ships.  This game will grow exponentially, and the combat will become far more complex.
Personally, I really like the overall capacity of the C11, as it is currently set up.  It is the premier carrier ship in SA.  When it shows up to a fight, it should definitively swing the balance of power.  Plus, there are only 130 of them.  There are over 50,000 small fighters, and over 30,000 X5s.  So the overall balance of power is overwhelmingly on the side of small ships.To be fair to its class and cost, the C11 ought to pack a hell of a punch in hangared firepower. I hope that is the final vision, but I am confident they will make the best compromise.

author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content:
The ship was advertised, sold, and paid for by people, as have a certain amount of weapons, hangers and such, of certain specific sizes.  The creator and original furnisher of the product, who chose to offer it for sale with these specific features, needs to deliver on that spec.
[5:58 PM]
Now if ATMTA wants to have a Commander-sized hanger on a C11 house a bunch of ships only up to a certain size, which does not include a capital ship, and passes a sensible - common sense test, as carrying more for example , medium-sized ships than a Capital hanger would carry, then fine, that would make some sense.  Maybe a C11's Commander-sized hangers only carry up to a certain number of Large vessels, and no Capital-sized vessels.  That would be fine.  The description of the C11 states that there are "docking mechanics" for Capital sized vessels.  It does not state that Capital-sized vessels fit inside any of the hangars.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
I have to admit I can see the irony in seeing a group of people asking for updated ship specs, and a group of people who are complaining some ships specs might be tweaked down based on the comments of a developer  

Ship specs are a hot  topic. The team wants to be very careful with updating those & with sharing updates before the game is even out.

The team is 100% aware that changing ship specs will lead to frustration (I remember discussing this with them about a year ago already, if not before that).

A few observations from the above discussion:

 Updated ship specs are fine, as long as each ship gets better? But, what if a ship you don't own gets more improvements than yours? Yeah.. probably also not something the complaining people will be happy with  

 As the team knows changing these specs is going to lead to frustration, they are super careful about it and put a great amount of thought into it. This is exactly the reason why they don't want to publish anything until they are happy with the (current) end-result. They want to make sure they put as many changes as possible in the upcoming publication, instead of changing things every few months going forward.

Now of course, we all (should) know that balancing updates will continue to occur regardless, as balancing on paper only goes so far. You need to also see how these ships behave "in the real".

If you somehow convinced yourself you bought an overpowered ship (value/vwap) that would stay that way forever, then my guess is that you have not been playing multiplayer games much. It's the job of the team to create balanced gameplay. Only that type of gameplay has a decent chance of finding adoption. This tends to be an ongoing effort throughout the lifecycle of a game for traditional MP games. To suggest they should not be able to change any specs between selling the assets (which was done before before they even had started to build, let alone finetune the gameplay) and before the first version of the game has even been published is mind-boggling to me. That is surefire way to shoot yourself in the foot regarding player adoption going forward. 

Keep in mind that the team sold a good portion of those ships before even starting to work on the gameplay that these would be used for. They did their best to provide as many details as they could based on their current thinking at the time.

I'm not arguing that the ships should be updatable forever, that is a different discussion.
But at this stage, where we have no game yet, and you are just theorizing about your ship's performance based on the team's early theorizing about how the ships may be used in the game they had yet to build, seems a little silly to me

They want to make sure they put as many changes as possible in the upcoming publication, instead of changing things every few months going forward. Now of course, we all (should) know that balancing updates will continue to occur regardless, as balancing on paper only goes so far. You need to also see how these ships behave "in the real". If you somehow convinced yourself you bought an overpowered ship (value/vwap) that would stay that way forever, then my guess is that you have not been playing multiplayer games much. It's the job of the team to create balanced gameplay. Only that type of gameplay has a decent chance of finding adoption. This tends to be an ongoing effort throughout the lifecycle of a game for traditional MP games. To suggest they should not be able to change any specs between selling the assets (which was done before before they even had started to build, let alone finetune the gameplay) and before the first version of the game has even been published is mind-boggling to me. That is surefire way to shoot yourself in the foot regarding player adoption going forward. Keep in mind that the team sold a good portion of those ships before even starting to work on the gameplay that these would be used for. They did their best to provide as many details as they could based on their current thinking at the time. I'm not arguing that the ships should be updatable forever, that is a different discussion. But at this stage, where we have no game yet, and you are just theorizing about your ship's performance based on the team's early theorizing about how the ships may be used in the game they had yet to build, seems a little silly to me

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: I hope in the future (maybe 1-2 years after SAGE release) ships aren't updatable. Having C11 v1 be priced a certain way in an economy then get nerfed is a bit silly. I think that if you build a new C11 though the changes could exist there in C11 v2. As long as figures are in the ballpark and not completely off. If a ship ends up 15% better than it's VWAP suggests the market will find that. Not sure if I've explained this very well.  Eventually all the "OP" ships get dusted in HRZ or become super rare OP version of a particular ship which is pretty cool imo.

author: bunthius [STAR]
content:
Whatever we land on, ship specs don’t change in isolation. If there are rule changes it effects all ship configurations. So the current perceived power hierarchy in ships will remain despite sweeping changes.

Until specs are officially published on their in game build the configs are in flux and even after than rebalancing is a necessary tool to save the economy from itself. At some point it’ll be full on dao votes though. We’re just not there yet.

author: [EMP] jith blade
content: now thats a fair way to deal with it, meaning if anyones game will fundamentally change in an unfair way they will have the chance to state that it would happen and argue for a less drastic change

author: [EMP] jith blade
content: once stuff is live

author: [EMP] jith blade
content: like the fact with the c11 there will be stuff like deployment times, plus the fact that the actual combat mechanics will be more complex by then as well as the need for certain player and crew skills to make it be able to function at its peak, sage v0-2 will not exactly have all that

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: U guys have eyes on this in detail with the balancing stuff while we just speculate of little bits of info. By general conceived power hierarchy you mean basically ship class and currently outward facing shown component/module specs? Or internal used metrics?  Mind touching base on what atomic mentioned with legacy ships and how this will effect changes in performance between legacy and non legacy ships? Or are these legacy cases just slight tweaks to the general rule for rest of the designs in order to not lose all the design work on those legacy ships?

reply.preview: U guys have eyes on this in detail with the balancing stuff while we just speculate of little bits of info. By general conceived power hierarchy you mean basically ship class and currently outward facing shown component/module specs? Or internal used metrics? author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: Correct, current published specs on the live item details page including the vwap. Those will always be respected. And changes to any individual ship will also create cascading adjustments to other ships to keep things in the same relative alignment that players currently experience.  The legacy versus non-legacy is purely cosmetic in the way ships will be built out in engine. For example, if a ship requires a cargo hold that wasn’t previously built in then it’ll just be a numbers thing rather than a physicalized cargo hold. We have lore to justify this and don’t plan to do major reworks as the game evolves as Star citizen has had to do. Learning from the path they’ve helped pave for us. Whereas new ships we will build the appropriate physical dimensions. But it’s an iterative process and we will always be improving and so diminishing returns will continually be assessed as we plug forward.

author: Matt | IntergalacticHerald
content:
I recently reached a milestone of posting 52 weekly recaps on Star Atlas on my website. Amazing to me upon reflection!  But, I would like to do something more interactive.  So, I thought of doing a podcast that is a roundtable discussion of the week in review of Star Atlas.  I was hoping to have one or two guests on the podcast and we go through the Star Atlas news of the past week and give our thoughts.  I was thinking less than 30 minutes.  To stay with the traditional podcast format, it would just be audio only.  Totally fine with people going by their aliases/usernames.  Anyone interested?  I figured Foundation Room members would have a lot of comments.   Please send me a message if interested.

One challenge I do see is coordinating a time.  I am only available for this effort on the weekend.  But, I also know we have community members all over the world which might make finding a common time difficult.  But, willing to give it a try.

author: Odicerous
content:
I dunno.  Some will get rich.  And some will get rekt.  Most will do neither.
If the game is great, tech billionaires, Saudi Princes, or Russian Oligarchs will buy million dollar ships just for the novelty or the prestige.
And a dirt poor Indonesian kid will own that ship four years later, by taking huge risks and grinding day and night.
But that is the outlier. The big draw is the opportunity for people to play a game they like, with real economic consequences.  That is groundbreaking.
For most people those consequences will be very small.  A few bucks.  Maybe 10. Maybe a couple hundred.  But that equals tens of millions across the metaverse.
I don’t underestimate the amount of $$ people will spend.  Hogwarts Legacy has earned over $1 Billion.  That’s one game.
Walk through a Vegas casino on a Saturday night, and count the number of people with thousands of dollars of chips in front them. Casually losing all of it, in a game they know they can’t win. Because they are having fun and really don’t care.
The amount of rich people who blow huge sums of money just to have fun is staggering.
IMO, they alone can support thousands of normal people who are happy to make a couple of bucks and upgrade their ships.

Not the most kid friendly language here though lol),https://docs.metaplex.com/programs/understanding-programs (Great intro with pretty solid links for further reading).,https://lorisleiva.com/create-a-solana-dapp-from-scratch (loris is great! He also came up with the structure of the metaplex docs and wrote that understanding programs page. Anchor has changed quite a bit since then, but it's still useful IMO), There weren't many docs back when I got into solana development so I ended up just learning by reading though existing open source solana programs (the original candy machine program was what I started with actually). Diving into the SPL codebase or something like the metaplex program library will give you a pretty wide range of Solana programs.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content: I dunno.  Some will get rich.  And some will get rekt.  Most will do neither. If the game is great, tech billionaires, Saudi Princes, or Russian Oligarchs will buy million dollar ships just for the novelty or the prestige. And a dirt poor Indonesian kid will own that ship four years later, by taking huge risks and grinding day and night. But that is the outlier. The big draw is the opportunity for people to play a game they like, with real economic consequences.  That is groundbreaking. For most people those consequences will be very small.  A few bucks.  Maybe 10. Maybe a couple hundred.  But that equals tens of millions across the metaverse. I don’t underestimate the amount of $$ people will spend.  Hogwarts Legacy has earned over $1 Billion.  That’s one game. Walk through a Vegas casino on a Saturday night, and count the number of people with thousands of dollars of chips in front them. Casually losing all of it, in a game they know they can’t win. Because they are having fun and really don’t care. The amount of rich people who blow huge sums of money just to have fun is staggering. IMO, they alone can support thousands of normal people who are happy to make a couple of bucks and upgrade their ships.

author: [GIA]Strictz9
content: I was listening to Atlas Theory and Arktype's latest podcast(Metaversed) so shout out to them.  But they mentioned an interesting idea of "play and earn" instead of "play to earn".  I really like the idea.  The main goal of the game should be to have fun and making money is a side benefit.  Pure play to earn games just feel scammy and pozni like to me.  I really like that meme of play and earn, perhaps that could be a new term we roll with in the future to make things a bit more palatable to traditional gamers.  What do you guys think of that?

Out of the solid projects(not counting the 90%scammy ones) out there, SA isnt the only one, others have actual gameplay too. Showroom isnt gameplay. Remember Jima, Lumina's brother made his own showroom alone, not exactly the same but not too far off what we currently have. That's 2 guys doing it on their free time. Out showroom was developed by a team for 60+ people. just saying

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: I really don't see the need for marketing until we get to the later, updated versions of Sage.  Right now, we have a very strong core of people dedicated to this game.  Once the bare bones version of Sage comes out, it will attract plenty of similar type people - ones looking for early stage games to get involved with.  The type of people we have, are the type we want.  Interested in creating an amazing game, not* Wen Atlas Pump?*  This existing group, and those who find us, will provide amazing feedback for early Sage.  And as Sage improves, so will the userbase.  By the time we get to V2, some aggressive marketing should start a landslide that we might not actually like....

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
Hey @everyone,

I wanted to take a moment to express my gratitude for your enthusiasm and support for Galia's upcoming movement test bed, which we'll also be coupling with an immersive social campaign.

In the coming weeks, we'll be sharing more details about what you can expect, including a Town Hall that we know you all have been waiting for. This will be worth the wait. In the meantime, stick with us on this journey. We're extremely excited for everything lined up this year.

Thank you for your patience and continued support. It means a lot to not only myself, but the rest of the team who's building out this incredible universe.
